title: The Enchiridion
author: Epictetus
summary: The Enchiridion is a concise Stoic manual compiled by Arrian from Epictetus’ teachings, aimed at guiding students toward philosophical self-mastery. Emphasizing distinguishing what is within our power—our judgments, desires, and choices—from what is not—wealth, health, reputation, and death—it prescribes practical disciplines: control of passions, acceptance of fate, and acting according to nature. Unlike the Discourses’ dialogic immediacy, the manual offers distilled rules for daily conduct, military metaphors, and exercises to attain inner freedom. Historically influential, the manual shaped modern moral thought by promoting autonomy, asceticism of emotions, and philosophy as a way of life altogether.

characters.name: Epictetus
characters.personality: practical Stoic teacher, ascetic, focused on ethics and self-mastery
characters.lifespan: born a slave, became free, taught in Rome, was exiled under Domitian and founded a school at Nicopolis

characters.name: Flavius Arrian
characters.personality: devoted student, diligent recorder and compiler
characters.lifespan: took down Epictetus’ talks in shorthand, published the Discourses and arranged the Enchiridion; later became consul

characters.name: Musonius Rufus
characters.lifespan: taught Epictetus in Rome and shaped his early philosophical training

characters.name: Nero
characters.lifespan: one emperor in whose household Epictetus served as slave to a distinguished freedman

characters.name: Domitian
characters.lifespan: during his rule philosophers including Epictetus were exiled from Rome

characters.name: Hadrian
characters.lifespan: associated with Arrian, who served in Hadrian’s circle and later retired after the emperor’s death

characters.name: Montaigne
characters.lifespan: recorded as having a copy of the Enchiridion among his books

characters.name: Pascal
characters.lifespan: noted in the introduction as violently rejecting Stoic pride

characters.name: Frederick the Great
characters.personality: military ruler who admired Stoic texts
characters.lifespan: said to have carried the Enchiridion with him on campaigns

characters.name: Anthony, Earl of Shaftesbury
characters.lifespan: used passages from the Enchiridion extensively during serious illness

characters.name: Francis Hutcheson
characters.personality: moral philosopher sympathetic to Stoic ideas
characters.lifespan: cited among Scottish thinkers influenced by Epictetus

characters.name: Adam Smith
characters.personality: scholar influenced by Stoic moral thought
characters.lifespan: named as among those who valued Stoic moral philosophy

characters.name: Adam Ferguson
characters.lifespan: mentioned as influenced by Stoic ethics

characters.name: Seneca
characters.lifespan: contrasted with Epictetus for his existential and psychological insights

characters.name: Marcus Aurelius
characters.lifespan: contrasted for translating Stoic doctrine into a ruler’s regimen

characters.name: G. H. Mead
characters.lifespan: used as a modern parallel to Epictetus’ dedication to human problems

characters.name: Diogenes
characters.personality: ascetic exemplar evoked by the text
characters.lifespan: used as a comparative reference for Epictetus’ practical Stoicism

characters.name: Socrates
characters.lifespan: invoked as a Stoic analogue in Arrian’s depiction of Epictetus

characters.name: Oskar Piest
characters.lifespan: credited as General Editor of the series in the edition

characters.name: Thomas W. Higginson
characters.lifespan: credited as translator of this edition

characters.name: Albert Salomon
characters.lifespan: wrote the included introduction describing the work’s history and influence

locations.name: Actium (near Preveza, site in Epirus, Greece)
